⠀⠀ ⠀⠀⠀ ⠀ ⠀ ⠀ ⠀⠀ ⠀Riven

⠀⠀⠀⠀⠀╰══• ೋ•✧๑♡๑✧•ೋ •══╯

⠀ ⠀⠀⠀⠀ ⠀⠀ ⠀Zodiac sign: Scorpio

⠀⠀ ⠀⠀ ⠀⠀⠀ ⠀⠀ Age: 19 years

⠀⠀ ⠀⠀⠀ ⠀⠀⠀ ⠀Height: 186 cm

Riven grew up in a rough, noisy neighborhood in Magix, where keeping your fists clenched and not showing weakness became second nature. Softness was a dangerous luxury there, so he learned early on to rely only on himself. Sure, there were friends, mentors, but life always seemed to throw curveballs, enough that he eventually decided a loner survives best.

This outlook—“trust no one, everyone’s in it for themselves”—is what drove him to the Specialists’ Academy. It felt like he would finally gain the strength he’d always lacked and finally control his life, even if it meant toughening up a bit. Riven was irresistibly drawn to risk and breaking rules. He practically lived on that emotional rollercoaster, constantly challenging himself and the world around him. Honestly, behind all the adrenaline and sarcasm, he was hiding a deep-seated insecurity that had always haunted him. The more intense his inner struggles, the harsher he became with others, almost as if he were testing everyone who crossed his path. Sometimes, it seemed like he created problems on purpose, just to feel the shock once again. At the Academy, Riven quickly stood out—not so much for following commands as for his knack for getting into trouble.

The instructors tried to “fix” his attitude, but after a few attempts, they simply gave up. He was the type who couldn’t be “reformed.” Riven always did things his way, refused to follow other people’s rules, and often found himself in sticky situations. But despite his unbearable attitude, he had leadership qualities and undeniable skills. People both admired and resented him because he provoked equal amounts of fascination and irritation. Behind his external harshness and disdain for others was a fear of becoming vulnerable again. His past, where trust ended in disappointment, left scars. He believed that if he opened up to anyone, he’d lose control and get hurt once more. So he kept his distance, even from those close to him, constantly testing their patience to see if they could endure his emotional games
